items.scenario: You are a graduate student writing your master's thesis under Professor Bennett. After reading her recent feedback on chapter two, you have several questions about how to restructure the literature review section before submitting the next draft.
items.direction: Write an email to Professor Bennett. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank Professor Bennett for her detailed feedback on chapter two.
Describe the changes you plan to make to the literature review.
Ask whether the revised structure should follow chronological or thematic order.
items.subject: Thesis chapter structure — feedback request

items.scenario: You are a junior analyst at a marketing firm where the manager just launched a cross-training program letting employees shadow other teams. After your first week shadowing the data team, you noticed several aspects worth keeping and a few worth changing.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Carlsen. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell Mr. Carlsen which parts of the program worked well in your first week.
Mention one specific obstacle you encountered while shadowing the data team.
Suggest an adjustment that could make the second week more useful for participants.
items.subject: Feedback on the cross-training program after week one

items.scenario: You are a longtime member of the Riverside Recreation Center, which currently offers art classes only on weekday evenings. Many working residents have asked about weekend options, and you want to share their feedback with the center's program director.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Holloway. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the demand you have heard from other working residents at the center.
Suggest a Saturday morning slot that would fit most schedules.
Offer to help recruit volunteer instructors if budget becomes a concern.
items.subject: Proposal for weekend art classes at the rec center

items.scenario: Your close friend Naomi has traveled to Iceland twice and you are planning your first international trip there this August. You can choose between a coastal driving tour or a glacier-hiking package and would value her honest opinion before booking.
items.direction: Write an email to Naomi. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the two trip packages you are considering for August.
Ask Naomi which option she found more memorable during her own visits.
Mention any constraints, such as budget or hiking experience, that might affect the choice.
items.subject: Help deciding between two summer trip options
